headings.note: Per user request, use Geist. Ensure it feels premium by keeping tracking very tight. Use white-to-gray text gradients for the H1.

mono.usage: Command palette, code blocks, overlines, statistics, metrics, labels, table headers

colors.guidelines: CRITICAL: No purple/violet gradients. Maintain a stark B&W structural theme similar to Vercel/Linear. Only use Emerald/Warning/Danger for specific trust/risk states.

layout.spacing: Generous spacing. Sections should be padded with py-24 to py-32. Internal container paddings p-8 to p-12.
layout.grid: The Technical Look. Expose the skeleton using border-collapse. Use Mode B (Control Room Grid) for the Solution section.
layout.borders: Minimal corner radius. Use rounded-md (6px) or rounded-sm (4px) to feel like serious infrastructure.

buttons.primary: bg-white text-black hover:bg-white/90 rounded-md px-6 py-2.5 font-medium tracking-tight transition-colors shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(255,255,255,0.1)]
buttons.secondary: bg-transparent border border-white/15 text-white hover:bg-white/5 rounded-md px-6 py-2.5 transition-colors font-medium
buttons.github_star: Flex inline layout with GitHub icon, 'Star on GitHub', and a monospace counter.

cards.base: bg-[#0A0A0A] border border-white/10 rounded-lg hover:border-white/20 transition-all duration-300
cards.glow_effect: For feature cards, add a subtle radial gradient mask or a 1px 'Tracing Beam' on hover.

audio_player.requirements: Custom UI required. DO NOT use native HTML5 <audio> controls. Build a player with Play/Pause button, a fake visual waveform (using vertical bars), a scrubber, and timestamp texts using Geist Mono.
audio_player.container: bg-[#0C0C0C] border border-white/15 rounded-full px-6 py-3 flex items-center gap-4 text-sm font-mono text-white/80 max-w-lg mx-auto

command_palette.style: bg-[#050505] border border-white/10 rounded-md font-mono text-sm p-4 overflow-x-auto text-white/80 shadow-inner. Top bar should mimic terminal controls (red, yellow, green dots).

comparison_table.style: w-full border-collapse text-left. Headers: font-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-wider text-white/50 border-b border-white/10 p-4. Cells: p-4 border-b border-white/5. Highlight AgentNotary column with slightly lighter background (bg-white/5).

images.description: 3D-feeling illustration for the right side of the hero section or centered beneath the typography.
Screenshot of the repository for the Embla Walkthrough Carousel.
Screenshot of terminal usage for the Embla Walkthrough Carousel.
Abstract dark grid texture. Apply as absolute background in 'The Problem' section with opacity-10 mix-blend-overlay to create a gritty 'Wild West' atmosphere.
Author headshot for the blog post section 'Why Agentic Trust is the Next Big Frontier in AI'.

interactions.scrolling: Integrate @studio-freight/lenis (or lenis) for smooth scroll momentum.
interactions.animations: Use framer-motion for smooth entrance. Elements should fade in (opacity 0 -> 1) and shift slightly (y: 20 -> 0) with a slow, ease-out transition. Stagger feature blocks.
interactions.microinteractions: Hover states on code blocks to reveal a 'Copy' icon. Button active states must scale down slightly (scale: 0.98).

specific_instructions_for_main_agent: Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='github-star-button', data-testid='copy-pip-command').
Hero title must be exactly: 'AgentNotary: The Trust Infrastructure for AI Agents'.
Section 2: The Problem ('Wild West' of autonomous agents, security risks). Use subtle Amber/Red accents and the background texture here.
Section 3: Solutions. Bento grid containing 4 features: Cryptographic Notarization, EU AI Act Compliance, Adversarial Fuzzing, Runtime Guardrails.
Section 4: Walkthrough Carousel using embla-carousel-react showing repo and terminal screenshots. Add Voice-over Audio Player below it.
Section 5: Comparison Matrix Table against LangSmith, Langfuse, Helicone, AgentOps.
Section 6: CLI Command display block (tabs for init, seal, guard, compliance, attack, bom, bench, replay).
Section 7: Deep-Dive Blog Post ('Why Agentic Trust is the Next Big Frontier in AI'). Present cleanly like a Substack/Linear release note.
Do NOT use any purple or violet colors. Strictly Vercel/Anthropic style.
